solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def checkInclusion(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> bool:
        if len(s1) > len(s2):
            return False
        
        from collections import Counter
        
        target = Counter(s1)
        window = Counter(s2[:len(s1)])
        
        if window == target:
            return True
        
        for i in range(len(s1), len(s2)):
            left_char = s2[i - len(s1)]
            right_char = s2[i]
            
            window[right_char] += 1
            window[left_char] -= 1
            
            if window[left_char] == 0:
                del window[left_char]
            
            if window == target:
                return True
        
        return False
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n + m)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses sliding window with fixed-size Counter comparison. Only 26 possible lowercase letters keep space O(1). Single pass through s2 with O(1) updates per step.
